home.description: I grew up in Cincinnati, Ohio. As a young child I would spend my days either buried in books or playing tennis. Growing up, I claimed to resent the piano lessons my mom took me to (secretly, I enjoyed them but don't tell her that). In high school I lost all 3 student government races I ran for, but discovered a love for mock trial.

calgary.description: Calgary is where my mom grew up and where me and my younger sister would spend part of our summers. I fondly remember the Calgary Stampede (the world's greatest rodeo) and the accompanying pancake breakfasts. Banff and the olympic park were other consistent highlights!

college.description: I went to college at the University of Virginia in Charlottesville! One of the highlights of my experience was living at 5 West Range, 4 doors down from where Edgar Allen Poe lived, wrote, and gambled as a student!

internships.description: I spent a couple summer in Cambridge, Massachussettes interning with Red Hat. It was a great chance to learn and grow! I especially loved eating at Zinnekin's Waffles and Nu Kitchen after climbing at Central Rock Climbing Gym.

firstJob.description: Washington, D.C. is where I live now. I moved here during the Great Pandemic of 2020 and never actually been to my office. In my world, DC is a quiet city with light traffic and pleasant bike rides. My favorite place in DC is the National Arboretum and is my first recommendation to any visitor. Currently, I work at American Forests as the Senior Manager of Data Science.
